colors.usage: App body background in Visual Shop.dc.html (main file). Redesign uses #eef0f4 for body.
Redesign body bg; also used as muted track/progress-bar background and empty bar fills in both files.
Cards, panels, search input, modal surfaces, KPI tiles, header bar (most frequent color, 137 uses)
Neutral pill background (Scheduled/Received/Received status), kbd hint bg, inactive toggle
Brand/primary: logo tile bg, active nav text, links, progress fill, primary action accents, 'Sent'/'In Process' pill text
Active/selected nav item background, info pill background ('Sent','In Process')
Round avatar/icon chip background paired with #3d63dd
Body text color (set on body), headings
Muted/caption text, placeholders, timestamps (2nd most frequent, 135 uses)
More muted text, search placeholder, kbd hint text, micro-labels
Faint section group labels (uppercase nav headers)

colors.role: text on neutral pill / nav idle
colors.usage: Idle nav item text, neutral status pill text (Scheduled/Received/rev C)
Primary border/divider (cards, header bottom-border, sidebar right-border)
Input borders, pill borders, secondary dividers
Success green: status pill text (Active/Won/Shipped/Released/Ready to ship), positive deltas, status dots
Completed timeline connector / success accent line
Warning amber: status pill text (On Hold/Draft/Approve), past-due text
Danger red: status pill text (Late), error counts (Late '5'), error focus ring rgba(212,80,62,.12)

typography.usage: All UI text. Loaded via Google Fonts IBM Plex Sans wght 400;500;600;700. Set on body.
Monospace for work-order/part IDs, KPI numbers, timestamps, status pills, uppercase section labels, logo glyph. Loaded wght 400;500;600.
List/screen titles (Schedule, Tracking, Shop Floor), letter-spacing -0.01em
KPI tile big numbers (86, 94.2, $418K). Smaller unit suffix at 13px.
Card headers (Shop Floor, Needs attention); redesign section heads 19px -0.01em
Default body / nav item / input text
Captions, sub-labels under KPIs, badge counts, avatar role
Sidebar nav group headers (SALES/PRODUCTION) and section labels, letter-spacing .1em (nav) / .06em (table headers), color #aab0bd/#9aa1b0, uppercase
Keyboard shortcut chips (⌘K) bg #f0f1f5 border #e2e5ec radius 5px
Redesign eyebrow chip: letter-spacing .14em, color #3d63dd, bg #eef1fd, uppercase

shadows: 0 0 0 3px rgba(212,80,62,.12) (error/danger input focus ring; primary equivalent would use blue)

layout.sidebarWidth: 252px (main Visual Shop.dc.html, fixed via width:252px flex-shrink:0). Redesign sidebar has no fixed px width (auto, padding:18px 14px).
layout.contentMaxWidth: 1060px (main dashboard content column max-width:1060px). Redesign hero/content max-width up to 1360px. Modals: 560px and 420px (max-width:90vw). Viewport preview 1320x860.
layout.notes: Root app shell: height:100vh; display:flex; overflow:hidden; bg #f6f7f9 (main). Layout = fixed sidebar (252px) + flex main column with sticky 60px header and scrollable content. Empty-state copy blocks max-width:240px. Card grids use grid-template-columns repeat(6,1fr) for KPI row, 1.55fr 1fr for two-column dashboard.

tokens.notes: IMPORTANT: Neither file uses CSS custom properties (--vars); the inline <style> blocks contain ONLY resets, scrollbar styling, and keyframes (vsPulse opacity pulse, vsShimmer, vsGrow). ALL design tokens are hardcoded as inline style attributes inside the <x-dc> templates. Fonts loaded from Google Fonts: IBM Plex Sans (400;500;600;700) + IBM Plex Mono (400;500;600). Mono face is the signature treatment for IDs (WO-48211), KPI numbers, timestamps, status pills, and uppercase nav/section labels. Two body-bg variants: main file #f6f7f9, redesign #eef0f4. Status-pill system is a 4-tone semantic set: green/#e8f5ef+#1f9d6b (positive: Active/Won/Shipped/Released/Ready to ship), blue/#eef1fd+#3d63dd (in-progress/info: Sent/In Process), amber/#fbf2e0+#c98a16 (attention/draft: Draft/On Hold/Approve), red/#fcebe8+#d4503e (error/overdue: Late), neutral/#f0f1f5+#6b7280 (Scheduled/Received/tags). Pills: mono 11px, border-radius 6px, padding 3px 9-10px. Active nav item: bg #eef1fd + text #3d63dd. Keyframe animations present: vsPulse (live status dots), vsShimmer (loading skeletons), vsGrow (bar charts, redesign). Screenshot 01 confirms: primary blue logo/active-nav, white cards on light-grey canvas, green positive deltas, red Late count, amber past-due. Source files: '/home/cojoa13/Documents/claude projects/HeatSynQ/Visual Shop.dc.html' and '/home/cojoa13/Documents/claude projects/HeatSynQ/Visual Shop Redesign.dc.html'.

entities.sampleData: CUSTOMERS: Apex Aerospace (Wichita KS, Net 30, #1042, YTD $214K, A/R $58,400, priceKey AERO-1, default cert AMS 2759/3); Midwest Gear & Axle (Toledo OH); Titan Fasteners (Cleveland OH, Net 45); Delta Turbine (Indianapolis IN); Crane Aero Components (Dayton OH); Vulcan Forge (Pittsburgh PA, Net 60, status Hold); Summit Bearings; Ironclad Tooling (Erie PA, Dormant). CONTACTS: Sara Lin/Buyer/sara.lin@apexaero.com/(316)555-0142, Marcus Reed/Quality Mgr/m.reed@apexaero.com, Ava Cho/A/P/ap@apexaero.com. PARTS: TS-4471 Turbine shaft 4140 Rc58-62 case .020-.030in; CS-88 Cap screw Rc40-45; BR-12 Blade root HV650+; RG-440 Ring gear; RC-200 Bearing race; DB-7 Die block; SP-119 Spacer ring 8620; HS-22 Hub shaft 4340; PG-88 Planet gear. PROCESS MASTERS: PM-CARB-58 (Carburize&temper rev C, 6 steps, used by 14 parts, created by T.Brand·Eng, updated May 2 2026), PM-VAC-44, PM-NIT-09, PM-CN-21, PM-NH-15, PM-ANN-03. RECIPE STEPS (PM-CARB-58): 1 Receive&verify/Receiving, 2 Wash&rack/Wash station, 3 Carburize/Batch IQ#3 [1700°F,8.0hr,0.90%C,Oil quench], 4 Temper/Temper Oven#4 [350°F,2.0hr], 5 Final inspect [Rc58-62,Case .020-.030in], 6 Certify&ship/Shipping. QUOTES: Q-2841 Apex $8,420 (Approve, salesperson S.Vance, valid Jul30, margin 42%), Q-2840 Midwest $3,150 Sent, Q-2838 Titan $1,890 Won, Q-2835 Crane $640, Q-2831 Vulcan $12,300, Q-2828 Summit $2,040 Won. QUOTE LINES Q-2841: Carburize per lb 600lb $6,180 + Temper per lot $1,440 + Certification flat $800 = $8,420. WORK ORDERS: WO-48211 Apex/TS-4471 Carburize+Temper 480pc due Jul2 In Process (PO 7741-A, ordered Jun26, recipe PM-CARB-58 rev C, 68% on Batch IQ#3), WO-48205 Titan 5,200 Vac harden, WO-48190 Delta Nitride 96, WO-48177 Midwest Carbonitride 310, WO-48142 Crane Temper 220 LATE, WO-48120 Summit Neutral 1,440 Ready to ship, WO-48098 Vulcan Anneal 40 Received, WO-48066/48041/48201/47980. EQUIPMENT/FLOOR: Batch IQ#3 (Carburize WO-48211 Apex 480pc 68% op K.Obi 1700°F 2h12m), Pit Furnace#1 (Nitride WO-48190 Delta 96pc 41% L.Day 975°F), Vacuum#1 (Vac harden WO-48205 Titan 5,200 88% R.Cole 1850°F 22m), Continuous Belt#2 (Carbonitride WO-48177 Midwest 310 55% M.Patel 1550°F), Temper Oven#4 (On hold/alarm +18°F WO-48142 Crane J.Ruiz), Endo Gen#1 (Idle). CERTS: C-9921 Apex WO-48211 AMS2759/3 Pending, C-9920 Titan AMS2759/2, C-9918 Delta AMS2759/10 Released, C-9915 Midwest AMS2759/7 Released, C-9910 Summit 'Customer spec SB-4'. SPECS: AMS2759/3 revK (SAE), AMS2759/2 revM, AMS2750 revF Pyrometry, MIL-S-6090 revA (DoD), SB-4 rev2 (Customer). STANDARDS: AS9100D (Quality, Mar2026), Nadcap HT (Process, Jan2026), ISO9001 (Feb2026), CQI-9 (Apr2026). INVOICES: INV-30412 Delta $6,740 Sent, INV-30408 Apex $11,200 Paid, INV-30401 Titan $1,560 Paid; uninvoiced: Summit WO-48120 $4,180 To bill, Midwest WO-48177 $2,910 To bill. A/R AGING: total $262K open / $31K past due; buckets Current $181K, 1-30 $50K, 31-60 $18K, 61-90 $8K, 90+ $5K. Per-customer: Apex $58,400 (ok), Titan $41,200 ($8,200 past 38d warn), Midwest $36,900, Delta $28,100 ($4,100 34d), Vulcan $24,600 ($13,400 past 47d bad), Crane $18,300. OPERATORS: Dana Mercer (DM, Plant Manager), S.Vance (sales, quote limit $25,000), T.Brand (Eng), K.Obi, L.Day, R.Cole, M.Patel, J.Ruiz. ROLES: manager/sales/office. PRICE KEY AERO-1 overrides: Carburize per lb $10.30 min $250, Neutral harden per lb $6.80 min $180, Temper per lot $1,440. DOCUMENTS: PO 7741-A.pdf (240KB), Quality agreement.pdf (1.1MB), TS-4471 rev C.dwg (820KB). REDESIGN-ONLY: CARs (CAR-0312 overdue 'Hardness out of spec'), Maintenance/Pyrometry (AMS 2750 TUS due Temper Oven#4 in 3 days), live SSI/SCADA furnace temps. Pipeline stages: Quote→Order→Schedule→Track→Cert→Ship→Invoice. Date context: Tue Jun 30 2026.

statuses: To bill (amber) — order shipped but not yet invoiced; invoice number shows as '—'
Sent (blue) — invoice generated and sent to customer
(subtitle counter: '9 shipped & uninvoiced' is the To-bill queue size)
fields: Invoice # (e.g. INV-30412; blank/'—' while in To-bill state)
invoice.fromOrder: Invoice is created per Work Order. Once a WO ships it appears in the Invoicing list as a 'To bill' row keyed to that WO with amount carried from the order, invoice # = '—'. Billing the row assigns an INV-##### number and moves it to 'Sent'. Strongly implies invoice-on-ship (the To-bill trigger is the shipped state), not invoice-on-completion. Amount = order value (which = quote total). No separate invoice line editing screen is shown.
invoice.arHandling: Sent/unpaid invoices roll into Accounts Receivable. A/R screen ages balances into buckets: Current, 1–30, 31–60, 61–90, 90+ days. A/R table columns: Customer, Balance, Current, Past Due, Oldest (days). Dashboard tiles: Open A/R $262K, Past Due $31K, Invoiced MTD $396K. A 'Close [month] period' action exists (period-close accounting). Past-due example: Vulcan Forge 47 days, customer flagged 'Hold'. Paid invoices drop out of open A/R.

fields: Parts list (each: part #, description incl. material, qty, spec/hardness Rc) — order can hold MULTIPLE parts: TS-4471 x480 + SP-119 x120
order.fromQuote: Order is created from a won quote. Order-detail Activity log literally records 'Order created from Q-2841'. Order value ($8,420) and the pricing breakdown are carried over verbatim from the quote's pricing lines.
order.pricingBreakdown: Pricing card mirrors the quote lines as a read-only breakdown: 'Carburize · 600 lb' = $6,180, 'Temper' = $1,440, 'Certification' = $800, Total = $8,420. Same numbers/labels as the quote pricing lines.
order.certHandling: Certification is flagged at the order header (Certify: Yes · AMS 2759/3). A dedicated cert card states: 'Cert AMS 2759/3 will generate after final inspect. Releases automatically on hardness pass.' Certs are their own entity (Certifications screen: C-9921 etc., status Pending→Released, linked to customer+WO+spec+type). An unreleased cert BLOCKS shipment ('Cert AMS 2759/3 required before ship'; dashboard 'X certs awaiting release · Blocking X shipments'). Certification also appears as a priced line ($800).
order.travelerModel: Process steps form the traveler and are instantiated from the part's assigned Process Master recipe (header: 'Recipe: PM-CARB-58 · rev C'). The Process Master defines standard ordered steps; each step has: number, operation, equipment/work-center, instructions, parameters (e.g. 1700°F / 8.0 hr / 0.90% C / Oil quench), and a track-in/track-out point. On the order, each step gains live state: done (✓ with operator + timestamp), in-process (◉ with % complete + est. finish), or scheduled/not-started. Step sequence on WO-48211 matches the PM-CARB-58 recipe steps: Receive & verify → Wash & rack → Carburize → Temper → Final inspect → Cert & ship.

statuses: Draft (amber) — shown on quote builder header
Approve / Awaiting approval (amber) — needs manager approval
fields: Part (id + name, e.g. TS-4471 — Turbine shaft)
Your quote limit ($25,000 — per-user approval ceiling)
Notes & terms (free text: lead time, validity, cert language)
Customer snapshot: Terms, YTD sales, Default cert, Open orders
quote.pricingModel: Line-item based. Each pricing line has a PROCESS, a BASIS, a QTY/WT, an editable RATE, and a computed AMOUNT (rate × qty-or-weight). Three basis types observed: 'per lb' (Carburize: 600 lb × $10.30 = $6,180), 'per lot' (Temper: 1 × $1,440 = $1,440), and 'flat' (Certification: 1 × $800 = $800). Per-piece is implied/likely but not shown. Note weight basis (600 lb) is distinct from piece quantity (480) — weight is entered/derived separately. Summary block: Subtotal $8,420, Discount $0 (discount field exists), Total $8,420, Est. margin 42% (margin shown but cost source not exposed).
quote.lineStructure: Columns: PROCESS | BASIS | QTY/WT | RATE (editable input) | AMOUNT. '+ Add line' button to add lines. Lines typically map 1:1 to processes/steps plus a certification line. The same lines flow through to the order's pricing breakdown.
quote.transitions: Draft → (Send quote) → Sent → Won. 'Save draft' and 'Send quote' are the two header actions. Quotes over the salesperson's quote limit ($25,000) route to 'Approve' (awaiting manager approval) before Sent. A Won quote spawns an Order (Activity: 'Order created from Q-2841'). Win/Loss and Quote Aging reporting implied.

openQuestions: Exact pricing formula: where do RATEs come from (part record, Process Master, customer pricing tab, or manual)? The customer detail has a 'Pricing' tab but its contract/price-list logic is not shown.
How is the per-lb WEIGHT (600 lb) derived from the piece quantity (480)? Is weight entered manually, computed from a part unit weight, or both?
Is a 'per piece' basis supported in addition to per-lb / per-lot / flat? Only three bases are demonstrated.
How is 'Est. margin' (42%) computed — what cost model feeds it, and is cost captured per process/lb? Cost data is not in this slice.
Discount handling: line-level vs quote-level, percentage vs amount, and who may apply it (field exists but is $0).
Approval rules: the $25,000 'quote limit' implies an approval workflow, but who approves, what happens above limit, and whether limits are per-user/per-role is undefined.
Who can send a quote vs. who must get approval first, and whether Sent quotes can be revised/re-sent (versioning).
Quote→Order conversion trigger: does marking a quote 'Won' auto-create the order, or is it a manual 'Create order' action? Does customer PO receipt drive it?
Invoice timing: confirm invoice-on-ship vs invoice-on-completion. The 'To bill' state is tied to 'shipped', but completion vs ship is not disambiguated.
Partial shipments / partial invoicing: orders hold multiple parts (480 + 120) — can parts ship and invoice separately, or only the whole WO at once?
Tax and freight: no tax, freight, or surcharge lines appear anywhere in quote/order/invoice — is the shop tax-exempt or is this simply out of scope?
Multi-part quoting: the quote builder shows a single part block while orders carry multiple parts — can a quote contain multiple parts/lines per part?
Credit hold enforcement: customers have a 'Hold' status (Vulcan Forge) — does hold block new quotes/orders/shipments, and at which step?
Certification auto-release rule specifics: 'releases automatically on hardness pass' — what defines a pass, and what happens on fail (rework loop, re-quote)?
Numbering schemes and currency/rounding rules for Q-, WO-, INV-, and C- identifiers are assumed but not specified.
A/R period close semantics: what 'Close June period' actually locks (invoices, cash receipts) is unspecified, and cash-receipt/application flow is not shown.

statuses.appliesTo: Order / Work order (also a Tracking column)
statuses.meaning: Parts/lot have physically arrived and been counted/verified at receiving, but no processing has started yet. Gray (neutral/queued) pill.
Work order has been assigned to a piece of equipment and a date on the load schedule, but the run has not begun. Gray pill.

statuses.appliesTo: Order / Work order (also a Tracking column and order-detail header)
statuses.meaning: Work order is actively moving through its heat-treat process steps on the floor. Blue (active/in-progress) pill.
Work is paused — typically due to a quality problem (e.g. failed final inspection) or a credit/hold issue; cannot proceed until cleared. Amber/red pill. Rendered as 'On hold' for equipment.
Work order is past its promised due/ship date. Red (error/urgent) pill; also surfaced as a 'LATE' flag on tracking cards.
All processing and certification are complete; the lot is finished and awaiting shipment. Green (done/good) pill. Equivalent tracking column: 'Available to Ship'.
The certification (e.g. AMS 2759/3 cert) has been released/approved, which unblocks the order so it can ship. Green pill. Auto-releases on a hardness pass.
Certification has been generated but is still awaiting release/sign-off; it blocks shipment until released. Amber pill.
Quote is being built and has not yet been sent to the customer. Used as the quote-builder header state.
Quote/invoice has been sent to the customer and is awaiting a response/payment. Blue pill.
Quote requires internal approval before it can be sent — e.g. it exceeds the salesperson's authorization limit ($25,000). Amber (action-needed) pill.
Quote was accepted by the customer and converted into an order. Green pill.
Order has shipped but has not yet been invoiced (shipped & uninvoiced). Amber (action-needed) pill.
Invoice has been paid in full by the customer. Green pill.
An invoice has been generated for a shipped order (used in finance metrics like 'Invoiced MTD').
Account/recipe is in active use and in good standing. Green pill. For a Process Master, 'Active' means the revision is the current one in use.
Customer account is on hold (e.g. credit/AR hold, such as a past-due balance); new work may be restricted. Amber pill.
Account is inactive — no recent orders (e.g. no orders in 60 days). Gray pill.
Equipment is actively processing a load right now. Green pill on the Shop Floor screen.
Equipment has no load and is available ('No load · available'). Gray pill.
The finished lot has been shipped to the customer (seen in customer order history).
Dollar value of quotes issued in the period ('Quoted MTD'); denotes a quote has been priced/issued.
Stage where parts are degreased/washed and loaded onto fixtures/racks before the thermal process.
Stage where surface hardness and case depth are verified on sample pieces before certification.
Finished, certified lots ready to be shipped (kanban equivalent of the 'Ready to ship' order status).
Result of final hardness inspection; a hardness pass auto-releases the certification, a fail puts the order on hold.

glossary.definition: Case-hardening process that diffuses carbon into the surface of low-carbon steel at high temperature (e.g. ~1700°F, controlled carbon atmosphere) then quenches, producing a hard, wear-resistant case over a tough core. Recipe example PM-CARB-58.
Case-hardening process similar to carburizing but adds nitrogen along with carbon to the surface, giving a hard case at lower temperatures; often followed by temper. Recipe example PM-CN-21.

Surface-hardening process that diffuses nitrogen into the steel surface at relatively low temperature, producing a very hard wear/fatigue-resistant case with minimal distortion (measured in Vickers, e.g. HV 650+). Recipe example PM-NIT-09.
Through-hardening in a neutral (carbon-balanced) atmosphere that hardens the part without adding or removing surface carbon; austenitize then quench. Recipe example PM-NH-15.
Hardening performed in a vacuum furnace to prevent surface oxidation/decarburization, giving clean, bright through-hardened parts. Recipe example PM-VAC-44.
Low-temperature reheat after hardening (e.g. 350°F, 2 hr, air cool) that reduces brittleness and relieves stress to reach final target hardness. Often a second step after carburize/carbonitride.
Slow heat-and-cool process that softens steel to improve machinability or relieve internal stress. Recipe example PM-ANN-03 ('soften for machining').
Sub-critical heat treatment that removes residual internal stresses from prior machining/forming without significantly changing hardness.
Hardening that develops the target hardness throughout the full cross-section of the part (vs. only the surface case).
Depth of the hardened surface layer produced by carburize/carbonitride/nitride processes, specified as a range (e.g. .020–.030 in) and verified at final inspection.
Hardness measured on the Rockwell C scale, the standard for hardened steel; specified as a range (e.g. Rc 58–62). Reported in the cert and checked on sample pieces per lot.
Vickers hardness scale used for thin/nitrided cases where Rockwell is unsuitable (e.g. HV 650+ for nitrided surfaces).
The shop production order for a job (e.g. WO-48211) created from a quote/order; carries the part, quantity, process, due date and travels the floor through track-in/track-out steps.
Printed routing/instruction sheet that accompanies a job (lot) through the shop, listing the process steps, equipment and operator sign-offs ('Print traveler').
A grouped batch of parts processed together under one work order; sampling for inspection is per lot (e.g. 3 pc/lot), and pricing/quantities are tracked by lot.
The fixture/basket parts are loaded onto for furnace processing (e.g. '2 racks of 240'); done during the Wash & Rack step.
Barcode/scan events that record when a job enters ('track in') and leaves ('track out') a process step or piece of equipment, driving live shop-floor status.

The master recipe (e.g. PM-CARB-58 rev C) defining the standard ordered steps, equipment, parameters (temp/time/%C/quench) and instructions; shared by every part assigned to it and revision-controlled.
Synonym used in the prototype for a Process Master — the defined sequence of steps and parameters for producing a required metallurgical result.
Integral-Quench batch furnace — a sealed atmosphere furnace with an internal oil quench tank, used for carburizing/hardening a batch then quenching without exposure to air (e.g. 'Batch IQ #3').
Deep vertical (pit-style) furnace loaded from the top, commonly used for nitriding and long parts (e.g. 'Pit Furnace #1').
Conveyor/mesh-belt furnace that moves parts continuously through heat and quench zones for high-volume processing such as carbonitriding (e.g. 'Continuous Belt #2').
Lower-temperature oven dedicated to the tempering step after hardening (e.g. 'Temper Oven #4').
Furnace that processes parts under vacuum to prevent oxidation/decarburization; used for vacuum hardening ('Vacuum #1').
Endothermic gas generator that supplies the controlled carbon-bearing atmosphere to atmosphere furnaces (e.g. 'Endo Gen #1').
Rapid cooling step (e.g. in oil) immediately after austenitizing/carburizing that transforms the steel to a hard martensitic structure.
SAE Aerospace Material Specification for carburizing and hardening of steel parts; a common customer-required cert spec in the prototype.
SAE AMS spec for heat treatment of low-alloy steel parts (used for vacuum/through-hardened jobs).
Additional AMS 2759-series sub-specs referenced for specific processes (e.g. carbonitriding, nitriding) on certifications.
SAE AMS specification governing pyrometry — temperature-measurement accuracy, furnace surveys and instrument calibration required for compliant heat treating.
U.S. military specification for the heat-treat process of steels used in aircraft.
A customer or industry document (e.g. AMS 2759/3, customer spec SB-4) that defines the required process, hardness, case depth and certification for a part.
An approved quality/process standard the shop is accredited to (e.g. AS9100D, ISO 9001, Nadcap HT, CQI-9), maintained in the standards library.
Industry accreditation program (National Aerospace and Defense Contractors Accreditation Program) for special processes such as heat treating.
Aerospace quality management system standard built on ISO 9001, required by aerospace customers.
AIAG Special Process: Heat Treat System Assessment — an automotive-industry self-assessment standard for heat-treat process control.
The quality document (e.g. C-9921) generated after final inspection certifying the lot met the specified process/hardness/case depth; must be released before the order can ship.

An individual ordered operation within a Process Master (Receive & verify, Wash & rack, Carburize, Temper, Final inspect, Certify & ship), each with equipment, instructions, parameters and a track-in/out scan point.
A pricing profile (e.g. AERO-1) tied to a customer/part that drives step pricing rates, minimum charges and overrides.
Pricing model where each process step is priced by a basis — per lb, per lot, or flat — with optional customer rate overrides and minimum charges.
The discipline of accurate temperature measurement and furnace temperature-uniformity control in heat treating, governed by AMS 2750.
Live, scan-driven view of jobs by equipment/area, organized into lifecycle columns (Received, Wash & Rack, In Process, Final Inspect, Available to Ship) showing real-time progress.
The weekly equipment-load plan assigning work orders to furnaces/ovens by day (e.g. Batch IQ #3, Pit Furnace #1 across Mon–Fri).
The steel alloy designation of the part (e.g. 4140, 8620, 4340), which determines the appropriate heat-treat process and achievable hardness.

screens.name: Today
screens.summary: Morning command center: KPI tiles (open orders, on-time %, revenue MTD, A/R, late orders), live furnace/floor status, needs-attention list, today's flow counts; Cmd+K command palette.

screens.name: Quotes
screens.summary: Open quotes table (Q-#, customer/process, value, age, status pill Approve/Sent/Won); rows click into Quote Builder.

screens.name: Customers
screens.summary: Active accounts table (name/city, terms, open count, YTD, status pill Active/Hold/Dormant); rows click into Customer Detail.

screens.name: Part Maintenance
screens.summary: Part records table (part #, description/customer, assigned process master, rev, hardness spec); rows click into Part Editor.

screens.name: Process Master
screens.summary: Process recipe library table (PM-#, description, step count, rev, parts-used-by); rows click into Recipe/Process Master Editor.

screens.name: Orders
screens.summary: Open work orders table (WO-#, customer/part, process, qty, due, status pill In Process/Scheduled/Late/Ready/Received); rows click into Order Detail.

screens.name: Schedule
screens.summary: Weekly equipment scheduling grid: rows are furnaces/ovens (Batch IQ, Pit, Vacuum, Belt, Temper), columns Mon-Fri with colored process blocks.

screens.name: Tracking
screens.summary: Kanban of WO travelers across stages: Received, Wash & Rack, In Process, Final Inspect, Available to Ship; cards show WO, customer, detail, scan time, LATE flag.

screens.name: Shop Floor
screens.summary: Equipment status card grid (Running/On hold/Idle) with process, WO, customer, qty, operator, progress bar, last scan.

screens.name: Certifications
screens.summary: Certs awaiting release table (C-#, customer/WO, specification AMS, process type, status pill Pending/Released).

screens.name: Specifications
screens.summary: Customer & industry specs table (spec id e.g. AMS 2759/3, title, rev, owner SAE/DoD/Customer).

screens.name: Standards
screens.summary: Approved standards library table (AS9100D, Nadcap HT, ISO 9001, CQI-9; title, category, last reviewed).

screens.name: Invoicing
screens.summary: Shipped & uninvoiced / invoices table (invoice #, customer, amount, date, status pill To bill/Sent/Paid).

screens.name: A/R
screens.summary: Accounts-receivable aging table by customer (balance, current, past-due, oldest age) with color-coded past-due.

screens.name: Reports
screens.summary: Report catalog grouped into Sales, Accounts Receivable, Production & Tracking, Quotes; each group lists named reports.

screens.name: Setup
screens.summary: Configuration card grid: Operators & Security, Plant Setup, Process Masters, Equipment & Areas, Pricing & Price Keys, Certifications & Forms.

screens.name: Patterns
screens.summary: Design-patterns / component reference screen (reachable from sidebar footer and command palette).

screens.name: Order Detail
screens.summary: Work-order detail view opened from the Orders list (route orderDetail); WO header, traveler/process steps and status.

screens.name: Quote Builder
screens.summary: Quote editor opened from a Quotes row (route quoteBuilder); build/price a quote, includes a dialog modal.

screens.name: Customer Detail
screens.summary: Tabbed customer record (Overview, Contacts, Parts, Orders, Documents, Pricing) opened from a Customers row.

screens.name: Part Editor
screens.summary: Part record editor opened from a Part Maintenance row (route partEditor).

screens.name: Process Master Editor
screens.summary: Recipe editor opened from a Process Master row (route recipeEditor); ordered process steps with equipment, instructions, params, tracking points.

screens.name: Redesign Option 1A — Workspace
screens.summary: Alt Today layout in Visual Shop Redesign.dc.html: sidebar (Today; Sales: Quotes/Customers/Parts & Recipes; Production: Orders/Schedule/Tracking/Furnace Floor; Quality: CARs/Maintenance/Certifications) + KPI row, live SCADA furnace floor, needs-attention, today's flow, command palette overlay.

screens.name: Redesign Option 1B — Pipeline
screens.summary: Alt Today concept with a top lifecycle/pipeline bar across order stages (static design exploration, no new module).

screens.name: Redesign Option 1C — Hub
screens.summary: Alt Today 'hub' concept (static design exploration, no new module).

nav.notes:
Main prototype = /home/cojoa13/Documents/claude projects/HeatSynQ/Visual Shop.dc.html. It is a single x-dc template (lines 9-786) driven by one DCLogic script (lines 787-1174); there is no per-screen data-props JSON — all mock data is inline in JS (buildLists() lines 843-962 for list screens; renderVals() lines 980+ for schedule/tracking/floor/ar/reports/setup; recipe steps lines 991-998; navData() lines 821-841 for the sidebar). Screens are switched via state.route, not separate components. Sidebar groups come from navData() (5 groups) plus two standalone footer links Patterns + Setup (template lines 51-52). The command palette (palAll, lines 1001-1025) enumerates the same destinations and adds Create/Action quick commands.

COVERAGE: The handoff's intended sidebar maps 1:1 onto the prototype with NO gaps. Every module is present — Today; Sales: Quotes/Customers/Part Maintenance; Production: Orders/Process Master/Schedule/Tracking/Shop Floor; Quality: Certifications/Specifications/Standards; Finance: Invoicing/A-R/Reports; Setup; Patterns. The prototype also exceeds the handoff with 5 detail/editor screens (Order Detail, Quote Builder, Customer Detail with 6 tabs, Part Editor, Process Master Editor) reachable by clicking list rows.

REDESIGN FILE = /home/cojoa13/Documents/claude projects/HeatSynQ/Visual Shop Redesign.dc.html is a smaller, SCRIPT-LESS static iteration (~323 lines) containing three side-by-side art-direction explorations of the Today dashboard only: Option 1A Workspace (line 38), 1B Pipeline (line 168), 1C Hub (line 245). It introduces alternate nav naming worth noting for the handoff: 'Parts & Recipes' (merged Sales item), 'Furnace Floor' (vs 'Shop Floor'), and a Quality group of CARs / Maintenance / Certifications (introduces CARs/corrective-actions and equipment Maintenance concepts not in the main prototype's sidebar), and it drops the Finance group from the visible nav. These are design variants, not additional modules.
